license_requirements: EU license holders generally do not require an International Driving Permit (IDP) to drive in France. Visitors from outside the EU are advised to carry an IDP alongside their national driving license. A full driving license held for at least one year is required by most rental suppliers. Young driver surcharges and minimum age requirements vary by supplier and vehicle category. We recommend confirming requirements with your chosen supplier before booking.
meta_title: Car Rental in France — Paris, Nice, Lyon & Airports
meta_description: Find car rental options across France. Explore pickup locations in Paris, Nice, Lyon, Marseille and at major airports including CDG and ORY.
meta_description_template: Find car rental options across {country_name}. Explore pickup locations in major cities and airports.
driving_notes: Traffic drives on the right in France. The priority to the right rule (priorité à droite) applies at unmarked junctions in towns and villages. Speed limits are 130 km/h on motorways in dry conditions (110 km/h in rain), 80 km/h on single carriageway roads outside built-up areas, and 50 km/h within towns. A warning triangle and high-visibility vest must be carried in the vehicle.
toll_information: The majority of French motorways (autoroutes) are subject to tolls, payable at péage stations by credit card, debit card, or cash. The Liber-t electronic toll tag is accepted at all toll lanes and is available through some rental suppliers on request.
fuel_notes: Petrol (essence) and diesel (gazole) are widely available across France. Electric vehicle charging is available at motorway service areas and in city centers. Fuel stations in rural areas may have limited opening hours.
parking_notes: City center parking in Paris and other major cities is limited and expensive. Underground car parks are available near major tourist areas. Blue zone parking requires a parking disc set to arrival time. Always read roadside signage carefully as parking rules change by street and time of day.
seasonal_notes: Peak rental demand in France occurs in July and August during the French summer holiday period. The ski season (December to March) creates strong demand in Alpine gateway cities. The Côte d'Azur sees extremely high demand in summer.
